source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/GeneralizedMethodOfMoments.cs @ fc28c0c ; RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Models/UnivariateDistribution/Bulletin17CDistribution.cs @ fc28c0c -- PROVISIONAL SELF-COMPUTED SMOKE VALUES (Task B11), NOT emitter oracles. The upstream GMM/B17C test files are structural-only, so no numeric GMM oracle has crossed into the fixture layer yet. These values were read back from the C++ core itself (a just-identified LP3 Bulletin17C GMM fit, Iterative strategy, BFGS optimizer + numerical Jacobian) and transcribed with loose tolerances; the point is that the new GMM/B17C path RUNS and reproduces the SAME numbers bit-for-identically across the C++/R/Python harnesses (identical compiled core). Task B12 REPLACES every value here with exact oracles emitted from the real C# GeneralizedMethodOfMoments/Bulletin17CDistribution and tightens the tolerances. The optimizer-dependent quantities (parameter) ride rel 1e-6; the finite-difference-sensitive covariance/standard_error/correlation/quantile_variance ride rel 1e-4 (a cross-compiler margin, per fixtures/README.md's tolerance policy loosened for B11 smoke); j_stat is ~0 for a just-identified fit (g(theta-hat) ~ 0), checked with an ABSOLUTE tolerance; j_stat_pval is NaN because the degrees of freedom q - p = 0 (a just-identified LP3 fit cannot test its own specification). simulated_value rides THIS case: after the fit, the estimator's best parameters are pinned into the B17C parent and a seeded (sample_size=8, seed=20250705) ISimulatable stream is drawn -- proving cross-language bit-identity of the seeded Mersenne-Twister draw off the fitted model (the short_exact digest precedent).
